```json
{
  "module": "ui-design-system",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 62,
    "domain_depth": 45,
    "data_richness": 40,
    "interaction_quality": 55,
    "ai_integration": 30,
    "mobile_first": 42,
    "performance": 58,
    "typescript_quality": 65,
    "error_handling": 52,
    "realtime": 35,
    "accessibility": 38,
    "security": 50,
    "differentiation": 45,
    "enterprise_readiness": 48
  },
  "overall_score": 47,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C2-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "Toast notifications lack ARIA live region and keyboard dismissal",
      "file": "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx",
      "description": "ToastEntry components render without role='alert' or aria-live='assertive'. Screen readers never announce toasts. Dismiss buttons lack aria-label. Toasts are not keyboard-dismissable via Escape key.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ToastEntry function component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The toast container div has no ARIA attributes, the dismiss button has no aria-label, and there is no keyboard listener for Escape. AFTER: (1) Add role='alert' and aria-live='assertive' to the outermost toast div element. (2) Add aria-label='Dismiss notification' to the dismiss/close button. (3) Add an onKeyDown handler on the toast wrapper that calls onClose(toast.id) when event.key === 'Escape'. (4) Add tabIndex={0} to the toast wrapper so it can receive keyboard focus. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C2-002",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "FolderBreadcrumbs truncation logic incomplete for mobile",
      "file": "src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx",
      "description": "The FolderBreadcrumbs component sets isMobile state and expanded state but the rendering logic after the early return for empty stack is cut off. On mobile with deep folder nesting, breadcrumbs overflow horizontally. The crumbButtonStyle has fixed maxWidth of 160px but no responsive adjustment for 375px screens.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, in the FolderBreadcrumbs component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The component has isMobile and expanded state but the render logic after the empty-stack guard is incomplete, and crumbButtonStyle uses a fixed maxWidth of 160px regardless of screen size. AFTER: (1) In the crumbButtonStyle, change maxWidth to '120px' when isMobile is true and '160px' otherwise by computing it dynamically inside the component. (2) When isMobile is true and stack.length > 2 and expanded is false, render only the first breadcrumb and the last breadcrumb with an ellipsis button in between that sets expanded to true on click. The ellipsis button must have aria-label='Show full path' and minimum touch target of 44x44px. (3) When expanded is true or stack.length <= 2 or not mobile, render all breadcrumbs. (4) Ensure the breadcrumb container has overflow-x: auto and flex-wrap: nowrap to prevent layout breaking. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C2-003",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "Error toasts auto-dismiss too quickly, missing retry action pattern",
      "file": "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx",
      "description": "Error toasts have TOAST_DURATION set to null (persistent) but a fallback auto-dismiss at 15 seconds. Per design standards, error toasts should persist until dismissed AND include a retry action button when the toast is triggered by a network failure. The 15s fallback causes errors to vanish before users read them on slow field connections.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ContextMenu.tsx, in the ToastEntry component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: Error toasts have a fallback auto-dismiss timeout of 15000ms (15 seconds) that fires unconditionally. AFTER: (1) Change the error fallback timeout from 15000ms to 30000ms (30 seconds) to give field users adequate time to read and act. (2) In the ToastEntry render, when toast.severity is 'error' and toast.action exists, render the action button with the label and onClick from toast.action, styled with the error text color and an underline, positioned to the left of the dismiss button. (3) The action button must have minimum touch target of 44x44px (minHeight and minWidth). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C2-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "visual_polish",
      "title": "Skeleton loaders missing from Primitives component library",
      "file": "src/components/Primitives.tsx",
      "description": "The design standards mandate skeleton screens matching exact content shape with pulse animation (opacity 0.3 to 0.7, 1.5s cycle). The Primitives file exports Card, MetricBox, Btn, Tag but no SkeletonCard, SkeletonRow, or SkeletonText primitives. Every page must build its own skeleton or shows no loading state at all.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/Primitives.tsx, add the following new exported components at the end of the file: BEFORE: The file exports Card, MetricBox, Btn, Tag but no skeleton loading primitives. AFTER: (1) Add a SkeletonPulse component that renders a div with CSS animation using keyframes for opacity cycling between 0.3 and 0.7 over 1.5s infinite. Use inline style with animationName defined via a style tag or use the animation property with a CSS-in-JS keyframe string. (2) Export SkeletonText: takes props width (string, default '100%') and height (string, default '14px'), renders a SkeletonPulse div with borderRadius 4px and background color '#E5E7EB'. (3) Export SkeletonCard: renders a Card-shaped div (borderRadius 12px, padding 24px, border 1px solid #E5E7EB, background white) containing three SkeletonText elements with widths '60%', '100%', '80%' and marginBottom 12px between them. (4) Export SkeletonRow: renders a div with height 48px, borderRadius 8px, background '#E5E7EB', with SkeletonPulse animation applied. All components must accept style prop for override. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "ui-design-system-C2-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "Breadcrumbs route labels missing for key construction pages causing blank breadcrumb text",
      "file": "src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx",
      "description": "The routeLabels map is missing entries for 'pay-apps' (shows undefined), 'portal' renders as 'Owner Portal' but child routes like 'portal/owner' produce doubled segments. Routes like 'change-orders' exist but clicking a breadcrumb segment should navigate to that route. The navigate callback is imported but usage in the route breadcrumb rendering is not visible in the truncated code.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/Breadcrumbs.tsx, in the routeLabels object, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The routeLabels map includes 'pay-apps': 'Payment Applications' and 'portal': 'Owner Portal' but is missing entries for nested routes and the label for 'pay-apps' uses a hyphen in the key correctly but the route matching may fail for paths like '/pay-apps/123'. Also missing labels for 'closeout', 'transmittals', 'permits', 'insurance', 'sustainability', and 'bim'. AFTER: (1) Add these entries to routeLabels: 'closeout': 'Closeout', 'transmittals': 'Transmittals', 'permits': 'Permits', 'insurance': 'Insurance', 'sustainability': 'Sustainability', 'bim': 'BIM'. (2) In the route-based breadcrumb rendering (if a component exists that reads from useLocation and maps path segments), ensure that when a path segment is a UUID or numeric ID, it is skipped or replaced with the entity title from context rather than rendered as raw text. If the route breadcrumb component does not exist yet, ensure the routeLabels additions are complete so any future breadcrumb rendering has all needed labels. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's design system is mature but dated, with heavy enterprise styling from 2018. Linear and Stripe set the bar SiteSync targets. Current SiteSync primitives (Card, MetricBox, Btn, Tag) cover basics but lack skeleton loaders, toast ARIA support, and responsive breadcrumb patterns that competitors like Fieldwire nail for field use. The absence of standardized skeleton primitives forces each page to reinvent loading states, creating inconsistency that Procore avoids with its internal Prism design system.",
  "summary": "The UI Design System module provides a solid foundation with theme tokens, Card/MetricBox/Btn/Tag primitives, toast notifications, and breadcrumb navigation. However, five significant gaps exist: (1) toast notifications completely lack ARIA attributes making them invisible to screen readers, violating both the accessibility acceptance criteria and WCAG 2.1 AA requirements; (2) mobile breadcrumb handling is incomplete, causing overflow on 375px screens; (3) error toast auto-dismiss timing is too aggressive for field conditions; (4) no skeleton loader primitives exist in the shared library, forcing every page to build custom loading states or show none; (5) breadcrumb route labels are missing for several construction-specific pages. These are all surgical fixes targeting existing files with precise before/after states. Scoring reflects that the design system has good TypeScript quality and reasonable visual polish but falls short on accessibility, mobile responsiveness, and the loading state infrastructure that every page depends on."
}
```
